top_stories.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing kit that breaks into Microsoft 365 accounts – no password required
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about a phishing kit called Kali365 that can compromise Microsoft 365 accounts without requiring passwords, effectively bypassing multi-factor authentication. The kit represents an adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) style attack that circumvents MFA protections, a core control relied upon by IAM professionals.

top_stories.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing-as-a-service platform targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The platform abuses OAuth device code authentication flows to steal session tokens and bypass MFA protections.

top_stories.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
top_stories.summary: The article examines MFA prompt bombing attacks, where attackers exploit push notification-based MFA by repeatedly sending authentication requests until users approve them. The piece focuses on how this technique bypasses traditional MFA protections and addresses implications for identity security strategies.

top_stories.title: Healthcare embraces AI while facing an identity security gap: report
top_stories.summary: A report reveals that most healthcare organizations expect AI-driven attacks targeting their identity infrastructure. Fewer than one-third of these organizations express high confidence in their ability to regain control if an AI agent exposed administrative credentials.

top_stories.title: Omada Named Overall Leader in KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass Report for Identity Governance and Administration
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has been named an Overall Leader in KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass Report for Identity Governance and Administration, receiving leadership recognition across Overall, Product, Innovation, and Market categories. KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass reports are widely referenced analyst evaluations used by organizations to assess and compare IGA vendors.

news.title: The CISO selling confidence in a market full of breach headlines
news.summary: Span CISO Hrvoje Englman spoke at the Span Cyber Security Arena conference about evolving security challenges facing enterprise IT. His remarks touched on AI coding assistants and autonomous agents being granted the same access privileges as their human creators, raising identity and access control concerns.

news.title: Nudge Security adds browser-based discovery for shadow AI agents
news.summary: Nudge Security has added browser-based discovery capabilities to its AI security platform to identify shadow AI agents on platforms lacking public APIs for agent identity and inventory. The feature targets AI agents built in tools like Airbyte and Atlassian Rovo, extending coverage beyond what API-dependent solutions can provide.

news.title: Companies built AI into core systems before figuring out how to govern it
news.summary: Check Point's 2026 Cloud Security Report found that 70% of organizations deploy GenAI in live environments, with 64% having AI agents in pilot or production, some with privileged access to core systems. Traditional security architectures designed for human users are struggling to govern AI systems that operate through APIs, automation, and autonomous actions.

news.title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
news.summary: Specops Software published guidance on enforcing strong Active Directory password policies while maintaining user experience. The article covers passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service password reset as methods to balance security and usability in AD environments.

news.title: Understanding acceptMappedClaims in Microsoft Entra ID Claims Mapping Policies
news.summary: The article explains how to configure the `acceptMappedClaims` property in Microsoft Entra ID claims mapping policies to resolve the AADSTS50146 authentication error. It covers enabling custom claims for application tokens within the Microsoft Entra ID platform.

news.title: JumpCloud Continues Its Global Leadership in the G2 Summer Reports
news.summary: JumpCloud announced it received 141 Leader badges in the G2 Summer 2026 Grid Reports, surpassing its previous record of 118. The recognition spans G2 categories relevant to JumpCloud's directory, SSO, and device management offerings.

news.title: Anthropic Expands Claude’s Enterprise Security Governance With 28 New Integrations
news.summary: Anthropic has expanded Claude's enterprise security governance capabilities with 28 new integrations, including Okta among other security vendors. The Okta integration is mentioned as one of many security tool connections, with the article primarily focused on Claude's broader enterprise security ecosystem rather than IAM-specific functionality.

news.title: What happens when security teams inherit identity
news.summary: At the Span Cyber Security Arena conference, Eric Woodruff, Chief Identity Architect at Semperis, discussed how organizations perceive identity and the challenges those perceptions create for security teams. The conversation focused on the organizational dynamics that arise when security teams take ownership of identity functions.

news.title: AI agents are creating a major security blind spot in financial services
news.summary: AI agents operating with excessive privileges are creating security and compliance risks in financial services. The issue centers on authorization and access management gaps as AI agents act autonomously without proper identity governance controls.

news.title: Manage machine identities: The hidden privileged access layer you need to manage
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of managing machine identities as a privileged access layer within organizations. It covers how automation, integrations, and workloads require authentication and permissions, creating a large and often unmanaged population of machine identities that fall under PAM and identity governance concerns.

news.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
news.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack campaign called TrapDoor has distributed credential-stealing malware across npm, PyPI, and Crates.io through more than 34 malicious packages spanning over 384 versions. The campaign began on May 22, 2026, with packages published in waves targeting developers across multiple ecosystems to harvest credentials.

news.title: 90 Days to Full NHI Management, Agentic AI Security and Operational Efficiency
news.summary: The article addresses the growing security challenge of non-human identities (NHIs) outnumbering human users, with governance frameworks struggling to keep pace. It outlines a 90-day framework for achieving full NHI management, covering issues such as stale credentials, limited visibility, and risks introduced by agentic AI systems.

news.title: Most organisations still store their passwords wrong. Here is what actually works.
news.summary: The article addresses why password storage practices remain inadequate despite the availability of password managers, noting that compromised passwords account for over 80% of hacking-related breaches. The piece covers what authentication and credential storage methods actually work for organizations, relevant to IAM professionals managing identity and access controls.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced "staged publishing" for npm, requiring maintainers to pass a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available for installation. The feature adds 2FA-gated controls to the npm publishing workflow as a supply chain security measure.

news.title: Working with Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article covers how to use Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID to customize token content for OAuth, OpenID Connect, and SAML tokens. It details adding user attributes such as first and last name to tokens and assigning these policies to Service Principals.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach now under congressional scrutiny. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials while lawmakers from both chambers demand answers.

news.title: Security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health - Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft has implemented security hardening measures to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health. The update addresses security improvements to the automatic upgrade mechanism for these identity synchronization and monitoring tools.

news.title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
news.summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026. The company received the highest scores in both the current offering and strategy categories in the evaluation.

news.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
news.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identities are creating new budget dynamics in enterprise identity security. The research highlights that managing, securing, and governing AI agent identities differs significantly from traditional IAM project approaches.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The repository, ironically named "Private-CISA," contained sensitive credentials, highlighting credential management and secrets hygiene failures within a major cybersecurity agency.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and secrets management failure.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. His admission covers SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on router flaws to silently siphon authentication credentials at scale.
